assessments.reasoning: The dispatch log confirms Aliyah as the assigned driver for the BR-Mon-A route on 03/13/2023 with no reassignment or swap. The log explicitly classifies the return leg as on-duty per the operations manual. Aliyah's recorded statement corroborates that he was returning to the terminal per required protocol. The police report confirms his CDL (A2241) matches the dispatch log. The residual 0.03 accounts for the remote possibility that undisclosed personal deviation on the return route could be raised, though the documented route and both the dispatch log and driver statement provide consistent, on-route evidence.

assessments.claim_text: Driver Aliyah satisfies the policy's driver eligibility requirements (valid CDL, age, and experience).
assessments.reasoning: The declarations page requires all scheduled drivers to hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14, be 23 or older, and have three years documented commercial driving experience. The dispatch log shows Aliyah's CDL #A2241-FL with an expiration date of 2024-08-31 (valid on the loss date). Aliyah stated in his recorded statement that he is in his fourth year with Northwind. His date of birth is 12/04/1975, making him 47 at the time of the loss. However, the declarations page specifies CDL Class B minimum; the police report records his license as 'FL CDL A2241.' A CDL Class A is a higher classification that meets or exceeds Class B requirements, so there is no eligibility deficiency apparent. The residual 0.08 reflects that the file does not contain the actual MVR or a verification that the annual MVR pull was completed, leaving a small gap that could surface an undisclosed suspension or endorsement issue.

The crash was caused by V-1 (Unit 14) rear-ending V-2 (Reyes's Civic). The citation against Aliyah for following too closely under FSS 316.0895(1) establishes a clear nexus between the covered vehicle's operation and the bodily injury claim. The residual probability accounts for the nascent medical picture — the injury is coded 'possible' rather than confirmed at this stage — and for the early stage of medical documentation.

outcomes.claim_text: Clean coverage — all coverage prerequisites are met; the exposure should be handled on the merits without reservation.
outcomes.reasoning: The loss date falls within the policy period; Unit 14 is a specifically described covered auto with liability coverage; the driver was on an authorized, on-duty return leg per the dispatch log and his own recorded statement; there is a documented bodily injury to a third party arising from the covered vehicle's operation; no obvious exclusion applies. The primary open item is confirmation of the annual MVR pull and the full policy form, neither of which presently signals a coverage problem.

outcomes.claim_text: Reservation of Rights — coverage is likely owed but a specific condition or potential exclusion warrants a written reservation while investigation continues.
outcomes.reasoning: A reservation posture could be appropriate if the annual MVR pull reveals a CDL suspension or disqualification that was undisclosed at policy issuance, or if review of the full CA 00 01 form and endorsements surfaces an exclusion not visible in the declarations. The driver eligibility provision requires an annual MVR pull; the file does not yet contain that document. Additionally, the full endorsement schedule (CA 99 03) has not been provided; its contents are unknown beyond the excerpt. These are investigative gaps rather than confirmed coverage problems, supporting a low but non-trivial ROR probability.

would_shift_distribution: Receipt and review of the annual MVR pull for Marcus Aliyah — a clean MVR would shift mass toward clean coverage; a suspended or disqualified CDL would shift mass toward ROR or denial.
Receipt of the full CA 00 01 policy form and all endorsements (particularly the CA 99 03 schedule) — any undisclosed exclusion applicable to this loss type would shift mass toward ROR or denial.
Medical records and treatment confirmation from Tampa General — confirmation of a diagnosed injury (vs. 'possible injury') is relevant to damages but does not change the coverage determination.
Evidence of any personal deviation on the return route — would shift mass toward ROR if the deviation is material and triggers a frolic/detour analysis under the policy's business use classification.
Confirmation that the signed UM rejection form was properly executed — relevant only if an underinsured motorist theory were raised by the named insured, not by the third-party claimant.

coverage_analysis_memo.body:
COVERAGE ANALYSIS MEMORANDUM

File:           CLM-2023-04471
Exposure:       EXP-2023-04471-REYES-BI
Policy:         NWL-CA-024471 (ISO CA 00 01)
Named Insured:  Northwind Logistics, LLC
Claimant:       Janet M. Reyes
Loss Date:      March 13, 2023
Reviewed:       January 31, 2025
Prepared by:    Argos Coverage Specialist (AI-assisted)

─────────────────────────────────────────────
I. COVERAGE IN EFFECT
─────────────────────────────────────────────
The policy was in force on the loss date. The declarations page establishes a policy period of 01/01/2023 12:01 A.M. through 01/01/2024 12:01 A.M. (DOC-001). The crash occurred at 16:42 on 03/13/2023 (DOC-002), which falls squarely within the policy period.

Liability coverage is written on Symbol 7 (Specifically Described Autos) with a combined single limit of $1,000,000 per accident and a $0 deductible (DOC-001).

─────────────────────────────────────────────
II. COVERED AUTO
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Unit 14 — a 2019 Freightliner M2-106 box truck (24-ft), VIN 1FVACWDT9KHKL4218 — is expressly scheduled on the declarations page with liability, comprehensive, and collision coverage (DOC-001). The police report confirms the involved vehicle is the same unit: 2019 Freightliner M2-106, owned by Northwind Logistics LLC, insured under policy NWL-CA-024471 (DOC-002). Vehicle identity is not in question.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
III. DRIVER AUTHORIZATION AND USE
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Marcus Aliyah (CDL #A2241-FL, exp. 2024-08-31) was the assigned and sole authorized driver of Unit 14 on 03/13/2023 for the BR-Mon-A route (DOC-004). The dispatch log expressly confirms: "No reassignment, no swap. Vehicle use during the return leg (Adamo → Causeway terminal) is on-duty per Northwind operations manual §4.3 (truck must be returned to terminal before driver clock-out)" (DOC-004).

Aliyah's recorded statement is consistent: "I was returning to the terminal. That's, uh, that's the protocol. Log the truck in, do the post-trip inspection, drop the keys" (DOC-003). The crash location — Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave — is confirmed on the inbound route from the final delivery at 4471 E Adamo Dr to the Tampa-Causeway terminal, with no indication of deviation (DOC-003; DOC-004).

The scheduled use class for Unit 14 is "pickup/delivery — general commodities" (DOC-001). The return-to-terminal leg following a completed delivery route falls within this use classification.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
IV. DRIVER ELIGIBILITY
─────────────────────────────────────────────
The declarations page requires all scheduled drivers to hold a valid CDL Class B, be 23 or older, and have three years of documented commercial driving experience, with an annual MVR pull by the producer (DOC-001). Aliyah's CDL is on file (Class A, #A2241-FL, valid through 08/31/2024 — DOC-004), he was 47 at the time of the loss (DOC-002), and he reported four years of employment with Northwind (DOC-003). A CDL Class A satisfies the Class B minimum requirement.

Open item: The current file does not contain the annual MVR pull. This is the primary investigative gap. Until the MVR is confirmed clean, a narrow basis for reservation exists.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
V. LOSS EVENT AND CLAIMANT INJURY
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Unit 14 rear-ended claimant Reyes's 2022 Honda Civic at the T-intersection of Causeway Blvd and Bermuda Ave. Aliyah was cited for FSS 316.0895(1) — Following Too Closely (DOC-002). Contributing factors: V-1 improper following distance (primary); no alcohol, drugs, or distraction (DOC-002). Reyes complained of neck and lower-back pain, was transported to Tampa General, and was coded 'Possible Injury (C)' (DOC-002). The causal nexus between the covered vehicle's operation and the bodily injury claim is clearly established on the available record.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
VI. POTENTIALLY APPLICABLE EXCLUSIONS
─────────────────────────────────────────────
No exclusion on the face of the declarations page presently applies. There is no indication of: intentional act; pollution (DOC-001 lists CA 23 17 broadened pollution, which is an extension, not an exclusion relevant here); workers' compensation; contractual liability outside the ordinary course; or impairment. The UM/UIM rejection (DOC-001) is irrelevant to this third-party bodily injury exposure. The full ISO CA 00 01 form and the CA 99 03 endorsement schedule have not been provided to this reviewer; a complete exclusion analysis is pending receipt of those documents.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
VII. LIMIT AND DEDUCTIBLE APPLICABLE
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Coverage Part: CP-NWL-2023-BI (auto BI, targeted by this exposure).
Limit: $1,000,000 per accident combined single limit.
Deductible: $0.
Note: The CSL structure means the $1,000,000 is shared across BI and PD arising from this accident. The PD coverage part (CP-NWL-2023-PD) carries a $1,000 deductible and $500,000 limit; any property damage component of the Reyes claim would be addressed under that part.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
VIII. OUTSTANDING INVESTIGATIVE ITEMS
─────────────────────────────────────────────
1. Annual MVR for Marcus Aliyah — obtain from producer (Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage) to confirm CDL validity and absence of disqualifying violations.
2. Full policy form (ISO CA 00 01) and CA 99 03 endorsement schedule — confirm no exclusions are triggered.
3. Tampa General medical records for Janet Reyes — required for damages assessment (not a coverage prerequisite, but needed for reserve-setting).
4. Any recorded or written statement from Reyes — standard intake.
5. V-1 post-accident inspection report and tow/repair records — may be relevant to any subrogation or comparative fault analysis.

─────────────────────────────────────────────
IX. COVERAGE PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────
Clean Coverage:  87%
ROR:             11%
Denial:           2%

The dominant probability is clean coverage given the strength of the currently available record. The ROR probability is driven exclusively by the pending MVR confirmation and the absence of the full policy form — both are retrievable items. The denial probability is residual and hypothetical on the present record.

ror_letter.body:
RESERVATION OF RIGHTS LETTER
[DRAFT — For Adjuster Review and Determination]

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Insured:        Northwind Logistics, LLC
      Policy No.:     NWL-CA-024471
      Claim No.:      CLM-2023-04471
      Date of Loss:   March 13, 2023
      Claimant:       Janet M. Reyes
      Coverage Part:  Auto Bodily Injury Liability (CP-NWL-2023-BI)

Dear Northwind Logistics, LLC:

[CARRIER NAME] ("the Company") has received notice of a bodily injury claim arising out of a traffic accident on March 13, 2023, involving Unit 14 (2019 Freightliner M2-106) operated by your employee Marcus Aliyah on Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, Florida.

The Company is conducting a good-faith investigation of this matter under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 (ISO CA 00 01 Form). While that investigation continues, the Company wishes to advise you of certain policy conditions and provisions that may be relevant to the determination of coverage, and to inform you that the Company reserves all of its rights under the policy, at law, and in equity, while it continues its investigation.

SPECIFIC RESERVATION BASES

1. Driver Eligibility — Annual MVR Condition

The Declarations Page of the policy states: "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience."

The Company's investigation confirms that Marcus Aliyah held CDL #A2241-FL (expiration 08/31/2024) as of the date of loss. However, the current file does not include the most recently completed annual Motor Vehicle Record (MVR) pull required by the above provision. The Company has requested this record from the producer of record, Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage, Inc. Until the MVR is reviewed and confirmed, the Company cannot fully evaluate whether the driver eligibility condition was satisfied at the time of the loss. In the event the MVR review reveals a basis for a coverage defense — including but not limited to a suspended, revoked, or otherwise disqualified license — the Company expressly reserves its right to disclaim coverage on that basis.

2. Full Policy Form and Endorsement Schedule Not Yet Reviewed

The Company's review has been conducted on the basis of the Declarations Page and the documents identified above. The full text of the ISO CA 00 01 policy form and the complete CA 99 03 endorsement schedule have not yet been reviewed in their entirety. The Company reserves all rights under any provision, condition, or exclusion of the policy that may be determined to apply upon completion of that review.

RESERVATION OF RIGHTS — GENERAL

The Company's decision to investigate this claim, and its continued handling of this matter, shall not constitute a waiver of, or estoppel with respect to, any policy term, condition, exclusion, or defense. The Company expressly reserves all of its rights under the policy and under applicable Florida law, including the right to deny coverage in whole or in part if the investigation reveals a basis for doing so.

You have an obligation under the policy to cooperate with the Company's investigation, including providing requested documents and making witnesses available for recorded statements. Please ensure that the annual MVR documentation for Marcus Aliyah is forwarded to the Company through your broker at your earliest opportunity.

If you have questions or wish to discuss this matter, please contact the undersigned claim specialist.

Sincerely,

[Claim Specialist Name]
[Title]
[Carrier Name]
[Address]
[Phone / Email]

cc: Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage, Inc. (Producer of Record)
    [Defense Counsel, if assigned]

NOTE TO ADJUSTER: This draft letter is predicated on two investigative gaps only: (1) the pending annual MVR pull and (2) the absence of the full policy form in the file. If the MVR returns clean and the full policy form is reviewed without identifying a new exclusion, this reservation basis may be resolved without the need to sustain a formal ROR posture. This draft does not constitute a coverage position or a recommendation.

Do not issue without independent legal and underwriting review.]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

      Date of Loss:   March 13, 2023
      Claimant:       Janet M. Reyes
      Coverage Part:  Auto Bodily Injury Liability (CP-NWL-2023-BI)

Dear Northwind Logistics, LLC:

[CARRIER NAME] ("the Company") has completed its investigation of the above-referenced claim arising out of the motor vehicle accident on March 13, 2023, involving Unit 14 (2019 Freightliner M2-106) operated by Marcus Aliyah on Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, Florida.

Based on that investigation, the Company has determined that coverage is not available for this claim under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 for the reason(s) set forth below.

[DENIAL BASIS — TO BE COMPLETED BY ADJUSTER UPON CONFIRMATION OF SPECIFIC GROUND]

Basis 1 (Driver Eligibility — Hypothetical): The Declarations Page of the policy provides: "All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience." Investigation has revealed that Marcus Aliyah [INSERT CONFIRMED FINDING — e.g., held a suspended CDL / was not a scheduled driver / did not satisfy the experience requirement] at the time of the loss. Because the driver operating Unit 14 did not satisfy the policy's driver eligibility requirements, no coverage is available under the liability coverage part for this accident.

Basis 2 (Policy Exclusion — Hypothetical): [TO BE COMPLETED upon review of full ISO CA 00 01 form and endorsements if a specific exclusion is identified.]

RIGHTS OF THE CLAIMANT / NOTICE

Pursuant to applicable Florida law and the terms of the policy, the Company advises that this determination affects the insured's right to a defense and indemnity under the policy for claims asserted by Janet M. Reyes. [CARRIER/COUNSEL TO CONFIRM FLORIDA DIRECT ACTION REQUIREMENTS AND CLAIMANT NOTICE OBLIGATIONS UNDER FLA. STAT. §627.4136 OR OTHER APPLICABLE PROVISIONS.]

The Company has acted in good faith in conducting its investigation. If you believe this determination is in error, or if you have additional information you wish the Company to consider, please contact the undersigned within [X] days.

All rights of the Company under the policy and applicable law are reserved.

Sincerely,

[Claim Specialist Name]
[Title]
[Carrier Name]
[Address]
[Phone / Email]

cc: Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage, Inc.
    Janet M. Reyes (or counsel of record)
    [Defense Counsel]

NOTE TO ADJUSTER: This denial letter template is provided for contingency purposes only. The denial probability on the current file is 2%, based solely on theoretical findings not yet in evidence. Issuance of this letter requires: (1) confirmed factual basis for denial; (2) review by coverage counsel; (3) compliance with Florida bad faith statutes (Fla. Stat. §624.155) and timely notice requirements. Do not issue this letter on the current record.

citations.text_excerpt: All scheduled drivers must hold a valid CDL Class B for Unit 14. Annual MVR pull by Producer. Drivers must be 23 years of age or older with three years documented commercial driving experience.
